arts.sum: Gabriel Martinelli grabbed his first Gunners goal for six months when he netted after 19 minutes, and just before the half-time whistle he set up Trossard to double our lead as the winger atoned for his red card last weekend at Manchester City. We began trying to restore our healthier lead and only a superb reaction save from Hermansen prevented Gabriel scoring a header from a corner, with the keeper also blocking Riccardo Calafiori’s follow-up before Timber blasted over, and then after Saka tricked his way into the area, Trossard saw a stabbed effort strike the post.

arts.ttl: Arsenal face contract dilemma with two Mikel Arteta stars in line to depart on free transfers
arts.sum: Thomas Partey has been back to his best in the centre of midfield for Arsenal this season but the Gunners are yet to show any clear appetite to extend his contract, meaning the Ghana international is currently in line to leave the Emirates Stadium on a free transfer in 2025

arts.ttl: Leicester boss Steve Cooper slams two controversies that benefitted 'lucky' Arsenal
arts.sum: Leicester suffered a gut-wrenching defeat to Arsenal on Saturday, with Foxes boss Steve Cooper left furious with referee Sam Barrott over two of the Gunners' goals

arts.ttl: Arsenal press conference: Mikel Arteta on Leicester drama, Nwaneri and Guardiola comments
arts.sum: With seconds remaining it appeared that Arsenal had somehow conspired to drop two points, but Trossard popped up again at the back post to poke in Saka's corner off Ndidi. It seemed as though the game would be a walk in the park Arsenal from that point but within a minute of the restart Leicester had one back as Wilfred Ndidi headed home from a free kick.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Bukayo Saka denied Premier League assist record despite Mikel Arteta plea
arts.sum: Bukayo Saka is denied a slice of Premier League history in Arsenal's 4-2 win over Leicester City, as the Gunners' third effort against the Foxes goes down as an own goal.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta fires blunt Arsenal Premier League title message after Man City blow
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has issued a blunt title response after Arsenal took advantage of Manchester City’s slip-up with a dramatic late Premier League win over Leicester City. The victory sees Arsenal now level on points with City thanks to a last-gasp 4-2 victory at the Emirates Stadium.

arts.ttl: Callum Hudson-Odoi says Arsenal have a £50m player who’s actually better than David Beckham
arts.sum: Callum Hudson-Odoi has been discussing some of the best wingers in Premier League history. The Nottingham Forest star was speaking to Mail Sport on TikTok...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta almost regrets 'something silly' claim as Leandro Trossard delivers for Arsenal
arts.sum: ARSENAL 4-2 LEICESTER: Last week's villain, Leandro Trossard, ensured he was this week's hero as the Belgian delivered in stoppage time by forcing Arsenal in front - via Wilfred Ndidi - to avenge his red card at Manchester City

arts.ttl: Callum Hudson-Odoi says Arsenal let go of a player who was even better than Phil Foden
arts.sum: Arsenal have let some incredible players go in recent years. The likes of Mesut Ozil and Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang were forced out of Arsenal under Mikel...

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Leicester prompts major Premier League title race change after Man City blow
arts.sum: Arsenal's last-gasp victory against Leicester City has seen them installed as the new favourites to win the 2024/25 Premier League title, with Manchester City's earlier slip up also factored into the matter. Also with six matches played, Chelsea sit one point behind their London rivals and City after a 4-2 win against Brighton this afternoon.
